concepts.detection_strategy: tree-sitter AST: detect 'loop' keyword nodes; traverse body to verify no 'break' statement exists; check condition expression is absent or always-true
false_positive_conditions: loop contains break statement (possibly conditional)
loop condition variable is mutated inside body
loop has counter increment that can exceed limit
tokio select/recv loops with shutdown signal

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Add a break condition or modify loop condition to eventually become false. Consider using 'while' instead of 'loop' when a termination condition exists.
review_questions: Does the loop have a guaranteed exit path?
Is the loop condition modified inside the body?

concepts.detection_strategy: dataflow AST: detect '/' or '%' binary ops; track denominator operand; check if zero is a reachable constant value for that variable; flag if denominator can be zero at any path
false_positive_conditions: denominator is checked with if x != 0 before division
denominator is a compile-time constant known to be non-zero
division uses checked_div() or saturating_div()

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Add a bounds check before division: if divisor == 0 { handle_zero() } else { a / b }. Or use a.checked_div(b) which returns Option.
review_questions: Can the denominator be zero at runtime?
Is there a guard check before division?

concepts.detection_strategy: tree-sitter AST: detect call_expr matching 'mem::uninitialized' or 'std::mem::uninitialized'; flag any use of the resulting value without proper MaybeUninit pattern
false_positive_conditions: Result wrapped in MaybeUninit and accessed via assume_init
mem::zeroed when zero is a valid bit pattern for the type
exclusions: MaybeUninit with assume_init after full initialization

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Replace mem::uninitialized() with MaybeUninit<T>::zeroed() or properly initialize the value. Use MaybeUninit<T> and call assume_init() only after all fields are initialized.
review_questions: Is the type Copy with a valid all-zero bit pattern?
Is MaybeUninit being used correctly?

concepts.detection_strategy: dataflow AST: detect unwrap()/expect() method calls on Option/Result; verify through dataflow if a Some/Ok value is guaranteed before the call; flag if None/Err path is reachable
false_positive_conditions: unwrap called after is_some()/is_ok() guard check
unwrap on result of parse() after validation
unwrap in test code
unwrap on known-good static strings

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Replace unwrap() with ? operator, unwrap_or(), unwrap_or_else(), or if let Some(x) = ... pattern. Add proper error handling.
review_questions: Is the Option/Result guaranteed to be Some/Ok at this point?
Should this use ? instead of unwrap?

concepts.detection_strategy: dataflow + constant folding: detect index expression on array/slice/Vec; compare against .len() or known size; flag if index >= len or index can be negative
false_positive_conditions: Access via .get(index) which returns Option
Bounds check with if index < len() before access
Loop uses iterators instead of index
Custom Index impl with different bounds semantics

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Use .get(index) instead of [index] for Option-based handling, or add a bounds check: if index < arr.len() { arr[index] } else { handle_oob() }.
review_questions: Is the index guaranteed to be within bounds?
Should this use .get() for safe indexing?

concepts.detection_strategy: control flow AST: detect code paths after return/break/continue; flag unreachable statements; detect match arms that can never be reached; identify if branches that always panic
false_positive_conditions: Code in #[cfg(test)] or #[cfg_attr(...)]
Match arms with panic! that are intentionally exhaustive
Generics with type bounds only satisfied in specific contexts

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Remove the unreachable code, or add #[allow(dead_code)] if intentionally kept for documentation or future use. Ensure match expressions have all cases handled.
review_questions: Is this code intentionally unreachable?
Should #[allow(dead_code)] be added?

concepts.detection_strategy: type-aware AST: detect comparison ops on f32/f64 types; track if either operand could be NaN (e.g., result of division, sqrt, parse); flag any direct == or != comparison as likely bug
false_positive_conditions: Explicit is_nan() check before comparison
PartialEq implementation that handles NaN specially
Test code with OrderedFloat or similar wrappers

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Use partial_cmp and handle Ordering::Less, Ordering::Greater, Ordering::Equal explicitly. Or use f32::is_nan() to check before comparison. For ordering, use partial_cmp().unwrap() only when NaN cannot occur.
review_questions: Can any operand be NaN at this comparison point?
Should this use f32::is_nan() first?

concepts.detection_strategy: AST + constant folding: detect arithmetic ops (+, -, *, /) on integer types without checked_/saturating_/wrapping_ prefix; flag literal overflow; track variable increments that could overflow
false_positive_conditions: Use of checked_add/saturating_mul/etc
Wrapping<T> types
Release builds where overflow is defined as two's complement

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Use checked_add(), checked_sub(), checked_mul() and handle the None case. Or use saturating_* for clamping behavior. Or explicitly use wrapping_* if wrap-around is intended.
review_questions: Is overflow possible here?
Should this use a checked arithmetic method?

concepts.detection_strategy: tree-sitter AST: detect .unwrap() and .expect() method calls on Option/Result types; exclude test modules and contexts where value is guaranteed
false_positive_conditions: unwrap in #[cfg(test)] modules
unwrap after is_some()/is_ok() guard
unwrap on result of safe parse with validation
unwrap in examples

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Replace with ? operator for propagation, unwrap_or(default), unwrap_or_else(|| ...), or proper match/if let handling. Use expect() only with a descriptive message in test/dev code.
review_questions: Is unwrap acceptable here (test/dev)?
Should this propagate errors with ? instead?

concepts.detection_strategy: AST + constant analysis: detect Regex::new() / RegexBuilder::new() calls with string literal; validate regex syntax at analysis time if possible; flag dynamic patterns that could panic
false_positive_conditions: lazy_regex! macro or regex! compile-time regex
Pattern validated with is_match() before Regex::new()
regex captured in a const with known-good pattern

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Use lazy_regex::lazy_regex! or regex::regex! for compile-time validation. Or validate pattern with Regex::new().is_ok() before using, handling the error case.
review_questions: Can the pattern be validated at compile time?
Should this use lazy_regex or regex! macro?

concepts.detection_strategy: AST pattern matching: compare for loop range bounds against container.len() or .iter().count(); flag i <= len vs i < len off-by-one patterns; heuristic-based for variable bounds
false_positive_conditions: Correct inclusive/exclusive range literals
Iterator-based loop instead of index-based
Custom iterators with correct semantics
Bounds check before array access

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Verify loop bounds match intent: for i in 0..n gives 0..n-1; for i in 0..=n gives 0..n. Check if comparison operator should be < instead of <=.
review_questions: Does the loop iterate the correct number of times?
Should the bound use = for inclusive?

concepts.detection_strategy: AST + scope analysis: detect explicit drop() calls; track variable moves and scope exits; identify when a value is dropped explicitly and then goes out of scope
false_positive_conditions: drop() on different variables
drop after mem::forget
Copy types that can be safely dropped multiple times

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Remove the explicit drop() call or ensure the variable is not used after scope exit. Use std::mem::forget if drop prevention is intentional.
review_questions: Is the explicit drop() call necessary?
Is the variable used after the drop?

concepts.detection_strategy: AST pattern: detect binary expressions mixing && and || without parentheses; flag a || b && c (&& has higher precedence) when || might be intended
false_positive_conditions: Explicit parentheses present
Simple unambiguous precedence cases
Test code with comments documenting intent

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Add explicit parentheses to clarify intended grouping: (a || b) && c instead of a || b && c. Prefer breaking complex boolean expressions into named variables.
review_questions: Is the intended grouping clear?
Should parentheses be added for clarity?

concepts.detection_strategy: AST + range analysis: detect binary comparisons on same variable with mutually exclusive ranges (x > 10 && x < 5, x >= 10 && x < 10); flag always-false and always-true conditions
false_positive_conditions: Different variables in each comparison
Float comparisons where NaN affects logic
Generic types with unknown comparable bounds

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Fix the contradictory condition logic. Likely a copy-paste error or incorrect operator. Check if one comparison should use a different variable or different operator.
review_questions: Is this condition actually contradictory?
Should one comparison use a different variable or operator?

concepts.detection_strategy: dataflow AST: track use-definition chains; detect variable writes that are never read before the next write; flag dead stores within basic blocks
false_positive_conditions: Variable reassigned in loop (accumulates result)
Variable used for debug logging between assignments
Side-effect expressions in assignment
Swap pattern (a = b; b = a)

agent_semantics.fix_playbook: Remove the redundant assignment if the first value is never used. Or initialize the variable with the correct value directly. Or use the first assignment's value.
review_questions: Is the first assignment value ever read?
Should this be a single assignment?
